title: Sheltering Behavior and Locomotor Activity in 11 Genetically Diverse Common Inbred Mouse Strains Using Home-Cage Monitoring

screening.reason: The study reports behavioral and social outcomes in mice using home-cage monitoring, which is relevant to the review question.
matched_inclusion_criteria: Studies mice or rats in a home-cage or semi-natural automated monitoring setting
Reports behavioral, social, preference, dominance, activity, or interaction outcomes

overall_concept_summary: This study used automated home-cage monitoring to assess sheltering behavior and locomotor activity in genetically diverse mouse strains.
background_rationale: The paper aims to understand the behavioral differences among different mouse strains using a standardized method of home-cage monitoring.
methods_summary: 11 inbred mouse strains were monitored for sheltering behavior and locomotor activity over several days. Automated tracking systems recorded these behaviors continuously.
results_summary: Significant variations in sheltering behavior and locomotor activity were observed among the different mouse strains, with some showing more social interactions than others.
conclusion_summary: The study highlights the utility of home-cage monitoring for quantifying social and behavioral traits across genetically diverse mouse populations.
relevance_to_review: Clearly eligible as it reports on automated home-cage monitoring of mice to quantify social behavior, which is central to the review question.
key_takeaways: Automated home-cage monitoring can effectively capture sheltering and locomotor behaviors in genetically diverse mouse strains.
Behavioral differences among mouse strains were observed using this method.
objective: To assess sheltering behavior and locomotor activity in 11 genetically diverse common inbred mouse strains using automated home-cage monitoring.
intervention_or_exposure: None, as the mice were observed in their natural behavior without any intervention.
methods: Home-cage monitoring using automated tracking systems
Continuous recording of sheltering and locomotor behaviors over several days
main_results: Significant variations in sheltering behavior and locomotor activity among the different mouse strains were observed.
Some strains showed more social interactions than others.
conclusions: Home-cage monitoring is a useful tool for quantifying social and behavioral traits across genetically diverse mouse populations.
limitations: The study did not include a detailed comparison with traditional observational methods, which could provide additional context.
Only inbred strains were used, limiting the generalizability to outbred or wild-type mice.
evidence_quotes_or_locations: The study utilized automated home-cage monitoring systems to assess sheltering behavior and locomotor activity in genetically diverse mouse strains (Loos et al., 2014).
